target: Mpemba Spectral Relaxation Theory x Amyloid Aggregation Vulnerability

rankings.title: Rough Energy Landscape Diffusion Asymmetry Creates the Spectral Signature That the Mpemba Index Detects
rankings.scoring_notes: Only SURVIVES hypothesis. All 4 GROUNDED citations verified correct. Provides the physical mechanism (Zwanzig roughness D_fold/D_misfold asymmetry) underlying the entire H1-H6 family. Clean falsifiable prediction via Sarle bimodality coefficient (BC > 0.555). Computationally demanding but feasible with existing MSM tools. Appropriately self-assessed at 5/10 confidence. +0.5 cross-domain bonus for crossing physics (roughness theory) + Markov spectral math + amyloid biophysics.

rankings.title: Mpemba Index of Protein Folding MSMs Predicts Amyloid Aggregation Propensity
rankings.scoring_notes: Flagship hypothesis with strongest novelty (zero PubMed co-occurrence for Mpemba AND amyloid). Core mathematical bridge (eigendecomposition of MSM transition matrices, Mpemba index as overlap count) is sound and directly transferable. WOUNDED by three citation errors: Robustelli 2018 is a force field paper not an MSM, Rosenman 2016 J. Mol. Biol. 428 does not appear to exist, Eschmann 2015 is in J. Phys. Chem. B not J. Chem. Phys. Groundedness penalized accordingly (4/10). Fixable with correct references in cycle 2. Bridge concept distinct from H5 (overlap integral vs. diffusion asymmetry). +0.5 cross-domain bonus.

rankings.title: Temperature-History Dependence of Prion Strain Selection Is Explained by Mpemba Eigenmode Branching
rankings.scoring_notes: Most ambitious hypothesis — novel application of eigenmode branching to the long-standing prion strain diversity problem. Highest domain distance (Mpemba physics + Markov spectral theory + prion biology). Scores highly on novelty, creativity, and impact. Severely limited by computational untestability: PrP^C to PrP^Sc involves massive conformational change beyond current MD capabilities, so the core MSM prediction cannot be verified. Groundedness penalty for gap between citations and predicted mechanism, plus cofactor dominance unaddressed. Bridge concept is distinct from H5 (eigenmode branching to strains) and H1 (overlap integral for aggregation propensity). +0.5 cross-domain bonus.

rankings.title: Non-Normal Liouvillian Dynamics of Amyloidogenic MSMs Create Transient Misfolding Acceleration Zones
rankings.scoring_notes: Intellectually stimulating application of non-normal transient amplification (established in fluid dynamics, Mpemba physics) to protein MSMs. The Henrici non-normality measure is correctly defined. Central obstacle: standard MSM construction (PyEMMA, MSMBuilder) enforces detailed balance, symmetrizes the transition matrix, and eliminates non-normality by construction — making the hypothesis untestable with any existing published MSM. Lapolla & Godec 2020 characterization is minor mischaracterization. Timescale relevance (ps-ns transient windows vs hours-to-days aggregation) also unresolved. +0.5 cross-domain bonus for fluid dynamics non-normality + Mpemba physics + protein biophysics.

rankings.title: Spectral Gap Ratio of Combined Fold/Misfold MSMs Predicts Amyloid Nucleation Lag Time
rankings.scoring_notes: Novel quantitative predictor (R = Delta_combined / Delta_fold) with a clear Spearman correlation target. Three major issues: (1) T_lag ~ 1/(1-R) is postulated without derivation and becomes negative when R > 1; (2) fundamental single-molecule vs multi-molecule gap — MSMs describe intramolecular conformational dynamics while nucleation is dominated by intermolecular secondary nucleation (Cohen et al. 2012 PNAS); (3) A2V mutation claim is factually wrong — A2V is pathogenic (accelerates aggregation in homozygous carriers), A2T is the protective mutation. Fixable in cycle 2 with a weaker rank-order prediction and A2T correction. +0.5 bonus for spectral theory + amyloid kinetics cross-domain bridge.

rankings.title: Comparative Mpemba Index Across Protein Pairs Identifies a Universal Spectral Aggregation Vulnerability Threshold
rankings.scoring_notes: Derivative of H1 applied as a binary classifier. Citations correct (all 5 GROUNDED citations verified). Two major weaknesses: (1) statistically underpowered — AUROC with n=12 proteins has bootstrap CI likely spanning 0.55-1.0, making the AUROC > 0.85 claim untestable; (2) binary amyloidogenic/non-amyloidogenic classification is biologically incorrect — FUS forms pathological aggregates in ALS/FTD and TTR-WT causes senile systemic amyloidosis in ~25% of individuals over 80, yet both are listed as non-amyloidogenic controls. Reframe as pilot continuous correlation study. Gains reduced cross-domain creativity bonus (same bridge as H1, lower conceptual novelty).

rankings.title: Inverse Mpemba Protocol Suppresses Amyloid Fibril Formation by Exploiting Eigenmode Decoupling
rankings.scoring_notes: Experimental protocol is the best-designed of all seven hypotheses (ThT fluorescence, multiple cooling rates, clear controls) and has highest translational potential. However, the entire mechanism rests on a FABRICATED empirical claim: Kusumoto et al. 1998 PNAS 95:12277 reports MONOTONIC Arrhenius temperature dependence (ke increases from 4C to 40C with EA ~ 23 kcal/mol), NOT the non-monotonic 'intermediate-temperature danger zone' claimed. Without non-monotonic temperature dependence, there is no eigenmode-overlap danger zone to bypass — rapid quench reduces aggregation simply by reducing time at high temperature (null hypothesis requiring no spectral theory). Groundedness penalty is severe (2/10). Ties H6 on composite; ranks 7th on tiebreak due to FATAL groundedness failure. Salvageable if reformulated around a protein with genuine non-monotonic nucleation temperature dependence.

top3_bridge_concepts.H5: Zwanzig roughness D_fold/D_misfold asymmetry as physical basis for bimodal eigenvalue spectrum
top3_bridge_concepts.H1: Mpemba index as overlap integral of conformational ensemble with slowest MSM eigenmode
top3_bridge_concepts.H7: Eigenmode branching from thermal initial conditions determines prion strain selection

elo_tournament.methodology: Round-robin pairwise comparisons on top-6 (H5, H1, H7, H3, H2, H6). Win assigned to higher composite score; margin noted for close comparisons.

pairwise_results.note: H7 wins by composite margin only; H3 has meaningfully better testability but H7 has higher novelty and impact

elo_note: Elo ranking (H5 > H1 > H7 > H3 > H2 > H6) is identical to composite ranking. No discrepancies to flag. Only marginal result: H7 vs H3 is 0.05 composite gap — the closest matchup. In Elo terms this is essentially a coin flip; H7's higher novelty and impact scores are the deciding factors.
